content.content:
1	// Copyright 2014 Manu Martinez-Almeida. All rights reserved.
2	// Use of this source code is governed by a MIT style
3	// license that can be found in the LICENSE file.
4	
5	package gin
6	
7	import (
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/mattn/go-isatty"
15	)
16	
17	type consoleColorModeValue int
18	
19	const (
20		autoColor consoleColorModeValue = iota
21		disableColor
22		forceColor
23	)
24	
25	const (
26		green   = "\033[97;42m"
27		white   = "\033[90;47m"
28		yellow  = "\033[90;43m"
29		red     = "\033[97;41m"
30		blue    = "\033[97;44m"
31		magenta = "\033[97;45m"
32		cyan    = "\033[97;46m"
33		reset   = "\033[0m"
34	)
35	
36	var consoleColorMode = autoColor
37	
38	// LoggerConfig defines the config for Logger middleware.
39	type LoggerConfig struct {
40		// Optional. Default value is gin.defaultLogFormatter
41		Formatter LogFormatter
42	
43		// Output is a writer where logs are written.
44		// Optional. Default value is gin.DefaultWriter.
45		Output io.Writer
46	
47		// SkipPaths is a URL path array which logs are not written.
48		// Optional.
49		SkipPaths []string
50	
51		// SkipQueryString indicates that query strings should not be written
52		// for cases such as when API keys are passed via query strings.
53		// Optional. Default value is false.
54		SkipQueryString bool
55	
56		// Skip is a Skipper that indicates which logs should not be written.
57		// Optional.
58		Skip Skipper
59	}
60	
61	// Skipper is a function to skip logs based on provided Context
62	type Skipper func(c *Context) bool
63	
64	// LogFormatter gives the signature of the formatter function passed to LoggerWithFormatter
65	type LogFormatter func(params LogFormatterParams) string
66	
67	// LogFormatterParams is the structure any formatter will be handed when time to log comes
68	type LogFormatterParams struct {
69		Request *http.Request
70	
71		// TimeStamp shows the time after the server returns a response.
72		TimeStamp time.Time
73		// StatusCode is HTTP response code.
74		StatusCode int
75		// Latency is how much time the server cost to process a certain request.
76		Latency time.Duration
77		// ClientIP equals Context's ClientIP method.
78		ClientIP string
79		// Method is the HTTP method given to the request.
80		Method string
81		// Path is a path the client requests.
82		Path string
83		// ErrorMessage is set if error has occurred in processing the request.
84		ErrorMessage string
85		// isTerm shows whether gin's output descriptor refers to a terminal.
86		isTerm bool
87		// BodySize is the size of the Response Body
88		BodySize int
89		// Keys are the keys set on the request's context.
90		Keys map[any]any
91	}
92	
93	// StatusCodeColor is the ANSI color for appropriately logging http status code to a terminal.
94	func (p *LogFormatterParams) StatusCodeColor() string {
95		code := p.StatusCode
96	
97		switch {
98		case code >= http.StatusContinue && code < http.StatusOK:
99			return white
100		case code >= http.StatusOK && code < http.StatusMultipleChoices:
101			return green
102		case code >= http.StatusMultipleChoices && code < http.StatusBadRequest:
103			return white
104		case code >= http.StatusBadRequest && code < http.StatusInternalServerError:
105			return yellow
106		default:
107			return red
108		}
109	}
110	
111	// LatencyColor is the ANSI color for latency
112	func (p *LogFormatterParams) LatencyColor() string {
113		latency := p.Latency
114		switch {
115		case latency < time.Millisecond*100:
116			return white
117		case latency < time.Millisecond*200:
118			return green
119		case latency < time.Millisecond*300:
120			return cyan
121		case latency < time.Millisecond*500:
122			return blue
123		case latency < time.Second:
124			return yellow
125		case latency < time.Second*2:
126			return magenta
127		default:
128			return red
129		}
130	}
131	
132	// MethodColor is the ANSI color for appropriately logging http method to a terminal.
133	func (p *LogFormatterParams) MethodColor() string {
134		method := p.Method
135	
136		switch method {
137		case http.MethodGet:
138			return blue
139		case http.MethodPost:
140			return cyan
141		case http.MethodPut:
142			return yellow
143		case http.MethodDelete:
144			return red
145		case http.MethodPatch:
146			return green
147		case http.MethodHead:
148			return magenta
149		case http.MethodOptions:
150			return white
151		default:
152			return reset
153		}
154	}
155	
156	// ResetColor resets all escape attributes.
157	func (p *LogFormatterParams) ResetColor() string {
158		return reset
159	}
160	
161	// IsOutputColor indicates whether can colors be outputted to the log.
162	func (p *LogFormatterParams) IsOutputColor() bool {
163		return consoleColorMode == forceColor || (consoleColorMode == autoColor && p.isTerm)
164	}
165	
166	// defaultLogFormatter is the default log format function Logger middleware uses.
167	var defaultLogFormatter = func(param LogFormatterParams) string {
168		var statusColor, methodColor, resetColor, latencyColor string
169		if param.IsOutputColor() {
170			statusColor = param.StatusCodeColor()
171			methodColor = param.MethodColor()
172			resetColor = param.ResetColor()
173			latencyColor = param.LatencyColor()
174		}
175	
176		switch {
177		case param.Latency > time.Minute:
178			param.Latency = param.Latency.Truncate(time.Second * 10)
179		case param.Latency > time.Second:
180			param.Latency = param.Latency.Truncate(time.Millisecond * 10)
181		case param.Latency > time.Millisecond:
182			param.Latency = param.Latency.Truncate(time.Microsecond * 10)
183		}
184	
185		return fmt.Sprintf("[GIN] %v |%s %3d %s|%s %8v %s| %15s |%s %-7s %s %#v\n%s",
186			param.TimeStamp.Format("2006/01/02 - 15:04:05"),
187			statusColor, param.StatusCode, resetColor,
188			latencyColor, param.Latency, resetColor,
189			param.ClientIP,
190			methodColor, param.Method, resetColor,
191			param.Path,
192			param.ErrorMessage,
193		)
194	}
195	
196	// DisableConsoleColor disables color output in the console.
197	func DisableConsoleColor() {
198		consoleColorMode = disableColor
199	}
200	
201	// ForceConsoleColor force color output in the console.
202	func ForceConsoleColor() {
203		consoleColorMode = forceColor
204	}
205	
206	// ErrorLogger returns a HandlerFunc for any error type.
207	func ErrorLogger() HandlerFunc {
208		return ErrorLoggerT(ErrorTypeAny)
209	}
210	
211	// ErrorLoggerT returns a HandlerFunc for a given error type.
212	func ErrorLoggerT(typ ErrorType) HandlerFunc {
213		return func(c *Context) {
214			c.Next()
215			errors := c.Errors.ByType(typ)
216			if len(errors) > 0 {
217				c.JSON(-1, errors)
218			}
219		}
220	}
221	
222	// Logger instances a Logger middleware that will write the logs to gin.DefaultWriter.
223	// By default, gin.DefaultWriter = os.Stdout.
224	func Logger() HandlerFunc {
225		return LoggerWithConfig(LoggerConfig{})
226	}
227	
228	// LoggerWithFormatter instance a Logger middleware with the specified log format function.
229	func LoggerWithFormatter(f LogFormatter) HandlerFunc {
230		return LoggerWithConfig(LoggerConfig{
231			Formatter: f,
232		})
233	}
234	
235	// LoggerWithWriter instance a Logger middleware with the specified writer buffer.
236	// Example: os.Stdout, a file opened in write mode, a socket...
237	func LoggerWithWriter(out io.Writer, notlogged ...string) HandlerFunc {
238		return LoggerWithConfig(LoggerConfig{
239			Output:    out,
240			SkipPaths: notlogged,
241		})
242	}
243	
244	// LoggerWithConfig instance a Logger middleware with config.
245	func LoggerWithConfig(conf LoggerConfig) HandlerFunc {
246		formatter := conf.Formatter
247		if formatter == nil {
248			formatter = defaultLogFormatter
249		}
250	
251		out := conf.Output
252		if out == nil {
253			out = DefaultWriter
254		}
255	
256		notlogged := conf.SkipPaths
257	
258		isTerm := true
259	
260		if w, ok := out.(*os.File); !ok || os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" ||
261			(!isatty.IsTerminal(w.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(w.Fd())) {
262			isTerm = false
263		}
264	
265		var skip map[string]struct{}
266	
267		if length := len(notlogged); length > 0 {
268			skip = make(map[string]struct{}, length)
269	
270			for _, path := range notlogged {
271				skip[path] = struct{}{}
272			}
273		}
274	
275		return func(c *Context) {
276			// Start timer
277			start := time.Now()
278			path := c.Request.URL.Path
279			raw := c.Request.URL.RawQuery
280	
281			// Process request
282			c.Next()
283	
284			// Log only when it is not being skipped
285			if _, ok := skip[path]; ok || (conf.Skip != nil && conf.Skip(c)) {
286				return
287			}
288	
289			param := LogFormatterParams{
290				Request: c.Request,
291				isTerm:  isTerm,
292				Keys:    c.Keys,
293			}
294	
295			// Stop timer
296			param.TimeStamp = time.Now()
297			param.Latency = param.TimeStamp.Sub(start)
298	
299			param.ClientIP = c.ClientIP()
300			param.Method = c.Request.Method
301			param.StatusCode = c.Writer.Status()
302			param.ErrorMessage = c.Errors.ByType(ErrorTypePrivate).String()
303	
304			param.BodySize = c.Writer.Size()
305	
306			if raw != "" && !conf.SkipQueryString {
307				path = path + "?" + raw
308			}
309	
310			param.Path = path
311	
312			fmt.Fprint(out, formatter(param))
313		}
314	}
315

content.content:
61	type Context struct {
62		writermem responseWriter
63		Request   *http.Request
64		Writer    ResponseWriter
65	
66		Params   Params
67		handlers HandlersChain
68		index    int8
69		fullPath string
70	
71		engine       *Engine
72		params       *Params
73		skippedNodes *[]skippedNode
74	
75		// This mutex protects Keys map.
76		mu sync.RWMutex
77	
78		// Keys is a key/value pair exclusively for the context of each request.
79		Keys map[any]any
80	
81		// Errors is a list of errors attached to all the handlers/middlewares who used this context.
82		Errors errorMsgs
83	
84		// Accepted defines a list of manually accepted formats for content negotiation.
85		Accepted []string
86	
87		// queryCache caches the query result from c.Request.URL.Query().
88		queryCache url.Values
89	
90		// formCache caches c.Request.PostForm, which contains the parsed form data from POST, PATCH,
91		// or PUT body parameters.
92		formCache url.Values
93	
94		// SameSite allows a server to define a cookie attribute making it impossible for
95		// the browser to send this cookie along with cross-site requests.
96		sameSite http.SameSite
97	}
98	
99	/************************************/
100	/********** CONTEXT CREATION ********/

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete picture of gin's logging system. Let me synthesize this into a comprehensive answer for the user. I'll cover:

1. The existing logging infrastructure
2. What's already captured vs. what's missing
3. The files that would need to change
4. The concrete implementation approach

Here's my analysis:

## Current state

Gin already has a functional request logging middleware in `logger.go`. The `LoggerWithConfig` function (lines 245-314) is the core, and it's invoked by:
- `Logger()` (line 224) — used in `Default()` in `gin.go:236-241`


- `LoggerWithFormatter()` (line 229)
- `LoggerWithWriter()` (line 237)

### What's already captured (`LogFormatterParams`):
The middleware already tracks the request object with all its headers and metadata, timestamps, response status and latency, client IP, HTTP method, request path with query parameters, any error messages from the context, response body size, and the request-scoped key/value store.

### What's missing for "comprehensive" logging:
I need to add request body size, extract request headers and user agent directly into the params, capture the route pattern (the template like `/users/:id` rather than the actual path), support request/trace IDs, move beyond plain text formatting to structured JSON output, and integrate with panic recovery and response headers.

### Files that would need to change:

**In `logger.go`** — I'd add fields for request body size, route pattern, user agent, and request ID to `LogFormatterParams`, then update `LoggerWithConfig` to populate these new fields and introduce a structured JSON formatter option.

**In `context.go`** — The `fullPath` is already available but not being used in the logger.  I should check if `c.FullPath()` is already exposed as a method since `LoggerWithConfig` has access to the context and can call it directly.

**In `gin.go`** — No changes needed since the default logger is already registered.

**New file: `logger_structured.go`** — I'll create this to handle JSON formatting.

Now I'm thinking through what's actually missing from the current logging implementation. The existing logger captures method, path, status, latency, client IP, errors, and response body size, but it's missing request ID/trace ID tracking and the route pattern itself.

To make this comprehensive, I'd need to extend the `LogFormatterParams` struct with fields for route pattern, user agent, request ID, and request body size, then update the logger configuration to populate these from the context. The main changes would be in `logger.go` to add these fields and extract them from the request, and potentially in `context.go` to support request ID generation or retrieval.

I'm confident that `c.FullPath()` is available on the context and `c.Request.ContentLength` gives me the request size directly. I could also create a separate JSON formatter in a new file if structured logging is needed, or optionally update the default logger in `gin.go` to use a more structured approach.

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Here's the full analysis:

---

## Current State: What Already Exists

Gin has a functional but **minimal** request logger in `logger.go`. The middleware chain is:

```
ServeHTTP (gin.go:662)
  └─ handleHTTPRequest (gin.go:690)
       └─ c.Next()  ← runs handler chain including Logger middleware
            └─ LoggerWithConfig closure (logger.go:275)
                 ├─ records start time
                 ├─ calls c.Next() to run downstream handlers
                 └─ after response: logs method, path, status, latency, clientIP, errors, body size
```

**Already captured** in `LogFormatterParams` (`logger.go:68-91`):

| Field | Source |
|---|---|
| `Request *http.Request` | full request object |
| `StatusCode int` | `c.Writer.Status()` |
| `Latency time.Duration` | `time.Now().Sub(start)` |
| `ClientIP string` | `c.ClientIP()` |
| `Method string` | `c.Request.Method` |
| `Path string` | `c.Request.URL.Path` + optional query |
| `ErrorMessage string` | `c.Errors.ByType(ErrorTypePrivate).String()` |
| `BodySize int` | `c.Writer.Size()` — **response** body only |
| `Keys map[any]any` | `c.Keys` |

---

## What's Missing for Comprehensive Logging

| Gap | Where to get it |
|---|---|
| Route pattern (`/users/:id`) | `c.FullPath()` |
| User-Agent | `c.Request.UserAgent()` |
| Request body size | `c.Request.ContentLength` |
| Referer | `c.Request.Referer()` |
| Request ID / Trace ID | `c.GetString("X-Request-ID")` (set by prior middleware) |
| Structured/JSON output | custom `LogFormatter` |
| Request headers | `c.Request.Header` |
| Panic details | `Recovery()` middleware — currently separate, not integrated |

---

## Files That Change

### 1. `logger.go` — Extend `LogFormatterParams` and `LoggerWithConfig`

**`logger.go:68-91`** — add fields to the struct:

```go
type LogFormatterParams struct {
    Request *http.Request

    TimeStamp   time.Time
    StatusCode  int
    Latency     time.Duration
    ClientIP    string
    Method      string
    Path        string
    ErrorMessage string
    isTerm      bool
    BodySize    int
    Keys        map[any]any

    // New fields:
    RoutePattern    string        // c.FullPath()  →  "/users/:id"
    UserAgent       string        // c.Request.UserAgent()
    RequestBodySize int64         // c.Request.ContentLength
    Referer         string        // c.Request.Referer()
    RequestID       string        // c.GetString("RequestId") or X-Request-ID header
}
```

**`logger.go:289-312`** — populate new fields in the `LoggerWithConfig` closure:

```go
param := LogFormatterParams{
    Request:         c.Request,
    isTerm:          isTerm,
    Keys:            c.Keys,
    RoutePattern:    c.FullPath(),                      // already available
    UserAgent:       c.Request.UserAgent(),
    RequestBodySize: c.Request.ContentLength,           // -1 if unknown
    Referer:         c.Request.Referer(),
    RequestID:       c.GetHeader("X-Request-ID"),
}
// ... rest of existing population ...
```

**`logger.go:167-194`** — update `defaultLogFormatter` to include new fields (or write a separate JSON formatter):

```go
// JSON structured formatter — register via LoggerWithFormatter(JSONLogFormatter)
var JSONLogFormatter = func(param LogFormatterParams) string {
    entry := map[string]any{
        "time":          param.TimeStamp.Format(time.RFC3339),
        "status":        param.StatusCode,
        "latency_ms":    param.Latency.Milliseconds(),
        "client_ip":     param.ClientIP,
        "method":        param.Method,
        "path":          param.Path,
        "route":         param.RoutePattern,
        "user_agent":    param.UserAgent,
        "request_id":    param.RequestID,
        "response_size": param.BodySize,
        "request_size":  param.RequestBodySize,
        "error":         param.ErrorMessage,
    }
    b, _ := json.Marshal(entry)
    return string(b) + "\n"
}
```

### 2. `gin.go` — Wire up request IDs in `Default()` (optional)

**`gin.go:236-241`** — `Default()` currently does:
```go
func Default(opts ...OptionFunc) *Engine {
    engine := New()
    engine.Use(Logger(), Recovery())
    return engine.With(opts...)
}
```

To add a request-ID middleware before `Logger()`:
```go
func Default(opts ...OptionFunc) *Engine {
    engine := New()
    engine.Use(RequestID(), Logger(), Recovery())
    return engine.With(opts...)
}
```

### 3. New file: `requestid.go` (if adding request-ID injection)

A minimal request-ID middleware that seeds `X-Request-ID` into the context before `Logger` runs:

```go
func RequestID() HandlerFunc {
    return func(c *Context) {
        id := c.GetHeader("X-Request-ID")
        if id == "" {
            id = generateID() // e.g., a UUID
        }
        c.Header("X-Request-ID", id)
        c.Set("RequestId", id)
        c.Next()
    }
}
```

### 4. `recovery.go` — Integrate panic info with logger (optional, more invasive)

Currently `Recovery()` (`recovery.go`) logs panics independently via `DefaultErrorWriter`. For unified logging, you'd pass a shared logger function into both:

```go
func RecoveryWithLogger(logger func(c *Context, err any, stack []byte)) HandlerFunc {
    return func(c *Context) {
        defer func() {
            if err := recover(); err != nil {
                // call c.Error() so the Logger middleware picks it up in c.Errors
                _ = c.Error(fmt.Errorf("panic: %v", err))
                logger(c, err, debug.Stack())
                c.AbortWithStatus(http.StatusInternalServerError)
            }
        }()
        c.Next()
    }
}
```

---

## Summary: File Change Map

| File | Change | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `logger.go:68-91` | Extend `LogFormatterParams` | Add route pattern, user-agent, request ID, referer, request body size |
| `logger.go:289-312` | Populate new fields in `LoggerWithConfig` | Wire `c.FullPath()`, headers into params |
| `logger.go:167-194` | Add `JSONLogFormatter` var | Structured output without breaking existing text formatter |
| `gin.go:236-241` | Add `RequestID()` to `Default()` | Seed request IDs before logger runs |
| `requestid.go` | New file | RequestID middleware |
| `recovery.go` | Optional: `RecoveryWithLogger` variant | Unify panic logging with request log |

### No changes needed in:
- `context.go` — `c.FullPath()`, `c.GetHeader()`, `c.GetString()` all already exist
- `routergroup.go` / `gin.go` routing — the middleware chain handles everything
- `handleHTTPRequest` (`gin.go:690`) — already sets `c.fullPath`

The cleanest approach for a library like gin is to **only touch `logger.go`** (extend the struct + populate in `LoggerWithConfig`) and ship a `JSONLogFormatter` alongside the existing `defaultLogFormatter`. Users opt into structured logging via `LoggerWithFormatter(JSONLogFormatter)`, and request-ID injection is a separate opt-in middleware. No callers break.

Now I have a complete picture. Here's the full analysis:

---

## Current State: What Already Exists

Gin has a functional but **minimal** request logger in `logger.go`. The middleware chain is:

```
ServeHTTP (gin.go:662)
  └─ handleHTTPRequest (gin.go:690)
       └─ c.Next()  ← runs handler chain including Logger middleware
            └─ LoggerWithConfig closure (logger.go:275)
                 ├─ records start time
                 ├─ calls c.Next() to run downstream handlers
                 └─ after response: logs method, path, status, latency, clientIP, errors, body size
```

**Already captured** in `LogFormatterParams` (`logger.go:68-91`):

| Field | Source |
|---|---|
| `Request *http.Request` | full request object |
| `StatusCode int` | `c.Writer.Status()` |
| `Latency time.Duration` | `time.Now().Sub(start)` |
| `ClientIP string` | `c.ClientIP()` |

---

## What's Missing for Comprehensive Logging

| Gap | Where to get it |
|---|---|
| Route pattern (`/users/:id`) | `c.FullPath()` |
| User-Agent | `c.Request.UserAgent()` |
| Request body size | `c.Request.ContentLength` |
| Referer | `c.Request.Referer()` |

---

## Files That Change

### 1. `logger.go` — Extend `LogFormatterParams` and `LoggerWithConfig`

**`logger.go:68-91`** — add fields to the struct:

```go
type LogFormatterParams struct {
    Request *http.Request

    TimeStamp   time.Time
    StatusCode  int
    Latency     time.Duration
    ClientIP    string
    Method      string
    Path        string
    ErrorMessage string
    isTerm      bool
    BodySize    int
    Keys        map[any]any

    // New fields:
    RoutePattern    string        // c.FullPath()  →  "/users/:id"
    UserAgent       string        // c.Request.UserAgent()

}
```

**`logger.go:289-312`** — populate new fields in the `LoggerWithConfig` closure:

```go
param := LogFormatterParams{
    Request:         c.Request,
    isTerm:          isTerm,
    Keys:            c.Keys,
    RoutePattern:    c.FullPath(),                      // already available
    UserAgent:       c.Request.UserAgent(),
    RequestBodySize: c.Request.ContentLength,           // -1 if unknown
    Referer:         c.Request.Referer(),
    RequestID:       c.GetHeader("X-Request-ID"),
}
// ... rest of existing population ...
```

**`logger.go:167-194`** — update `defaultLogFormatter` to include new fields (or write a separate JSON formatter):

```go
// JSON structured formatter — register via LoggerWithFormatter(JSONLogFormatter)
var JSONLogFormatter = func(param LogFormatterParams) string {
    entry := map[string]any{
        "time":          param.TimeStamp.Format(time.RFC3339),
        "status":        param.StatusCode,
        "latency_ms":    param.Latency.Milliseconds(),
        "client_ip":     param.ClientIP,
        "method":        param.Method,
        "path":          param.Path,
        "route":         param.RoutePattern,
        "user_agent":    param.UserAgent,
        "request_id":    param.RequestID,
        "response_size": param.BodySize,
        "request_size":  param.RequestBodySize,
        "error":         param.ErrorMessage,
    }
    b, _ := json.Marshal(entry)
    return string(b) + "\n"
}
```

### 2. `gin.go` — Wire up request IDs in `Default()` (optional)

**`gin.go:236-241`** — `Default()` currently does:
```go
func Default(opts ...OptionFunc) *Engine {
    engine := New()
    engine.Use(Logger(), Recovery())
    return engine.With(opts...)
}
```

To add a request-ID middleware before `Logger()`:
```go
func Default(opts ...OptionFunc) *Engine {
    engine := New()
    engine.Use(RequestID(), Logger(), Recovery())
    return engine.With(opts...)
}
```

### 3. New file: `requestid.go` (if adding request-ID injection)

A minimal request-ID middleware that seeds `X-Request-ID` into the context before `Logger` runs:

```go
func RequestID() HandlerFunc {
    return func(c *Context) {
        id := c.GetHeader("X-Request-ID")
        if id == "" {
            id = generateID() // e.g., a UUID
        }
        c.Header("X-Request-ID", id)
        c.Set("RequestId", id)
        c.Next()
    }
}
```

### 4. `recovery.go` — Integrate panic info with logger (optional, more invasive)

Currently `Recovery()` (`recovery.go`) logs panics independently via `DefaultErrorWriter`. For unified logging, you'd pass a shared logger function into both:

```go
func RecoveryWithLogger(logger func(c *Context, err any, stack []byte)) HandlerFunc {
    return func(c *Context) {
        defer func() {
            if err := recover(); err != nil {
                // call c.Error() so the Logger middleware picks it up in c.Errors
                _ = c.Error(fmt.Errorf("panic: %v", err))
                logger(c, err, debug.Stack())
                c.AbortWithStatus(http.StatusInternalServerError)
            }
        }()
        c.Next()
    }
}
```

---

## Summary: File Change Map

| File | Change | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `logger.go:68-91` | Extend `LogFormatterParams` | Add route pattern, user-agent, request ID, referer, request body size |
| `logger.go:289-312` | Populate new fields in `LoggerWithConfig` | Wire `c.FullPath()`, headers into params |
| `logger.go:167-194` | Add `JSONLogFormatter` var | Structured output without breaking existing text formatter |
| `gin.go:236-241` | Add `RequestID()` to `Default()` | Seed request IDs before logger runs |
| `requestid.go` | New file | RequestID middleware |
| `recovery.go` | Optional: `RecoveryWithLogger` variant | Unify panic logging with request log |

### No changes needed in:
- `context.go` — `c.FullPath()`, `c.GetHeader()`, `c.GetString()` all already exist
- `routergroup.go` / `gin.go` routing — the middleware chain handles everything
- `handleHTTPRequest` (`gin.go:690`) — already sets `c.fullPath`

The cleanest approach for a library like gin is to **only touch `logger.go`** (extend the struct + populate in `LoggerWithConfig`) and ship a `JSONLogFormatter` alongside the existing `defaultLogFormatter`. Users opt into structured logging via `LoggerWithFormatter(JSONLogFormatter)`, and request-ID injection is a separate opt-in middleware. No callers break.
